headline: Oil Prices Fall. Trump Says Iran War Going ‘Swimmingly’ and Should End Soon.
summary: Oil prices were falling Friday as a 10-day cease-fire between Israel and Lebanon came into effect and President Donald Trump sounded optimistic about a deal with Iran.  Brent crude futures  the international benchmark, fell 3% to $96.3 a barrel, while  West Texas Intermediate  futures slipped 3.7% to $87.7—on track to settle below $90 for the first time since March 23.  WTI futures have fallen 12% in April but remain 55% up so far this year.

content.title: Oil Prices Fall. Trump Says Iran War Going ‘Swimmingly’ and Should End Soon.
content.summary: Oil prices were falling Friday as a 10-day cease-fire between Israel and Lebanon came into effect and President Donald Trump sounded optimistic about a deal with Iran.  Brent crude futures  the international benchmark, fell 3% to $96.3 a barrel, while  West Texas Intermediate  futures slipped 3.7% to $87.7—on track to settle below $90 for the first time since March 23.  WTI futures have fallen 12% in April but remain 55% up so far this year.

headline: Energy Stocks Lose Wartime Gains
summary: U.S. energy stocks are falling on reports that the Strait of Hormuz was open to commercial traffic, further erasing gains from the first weeks of the war with Iran. Shares in fuel-refiner Phillips 66 and major oil companies Chevron and Exxon Mobil were all down about 6% in morning trading, which followed announcements from President Trump and the Iranian foreign minister declaring the strait open.

content.title: Energy Stocks Lose Wartime Gains

headline: Oil Prices Sink on Strait of Hormuz Reopening. What’s in the ‘Fine Print.’
summary: Oil prices were plunging Friday as Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz fully open to commercial traffic for the duration of the cease-fire between Israel and Lebanon.  Brent crude futures  the international benchmark, fell 10.5% to $88.92 a barrel, while  West Texas Intermediate  futures slipped 11.1% to $81.08—on track to settle below $90 for the first time since March 23 and on pace for its lowest close since March 10.  “The fine print is important,” RBC Capital Markets’ Helima Croft told Barron’s. “While the market continues to price a full resumption of flows, a number of shippers/insurers may be reluctant to rush back in with Iran having a final say on passage.”

content.title: Oil Prices Sink on Strait of Hormuz Reopening. What’s in the ‘Fine Print.’
content.summary: Oil prices were plunging Friday as Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz fully open to commercial traffic for the duration of the cease-fire between Israel and Lebanon.  Brent crude futures  the international benchmark, fell 10.5% to $88.92 a barrel, while  West Texas Intermediate  futures slipped 11.1% to $81.08—on track to settle below $90 for the first time since March 23 and on pace for its lowest close since March 10.  “The fine print is important,” RBC Capital Markets’ Helima Croft told Barron’s. “While the market continues to price a full resumption of flows, a number of shippers/insurers may be reluctant to rush back in with Iran having a final say on passage.”

headline: Buy This ETF Before the Empty Oil Tankers Reach U.S. Shores

headline: Dow jumps 800 points, S&P 500 at new record, Nasdaq gains 6.8% this week
summary: All three of the market indexes (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) closed Friday's session with significant gains, led by the Dow Jones Industrial Average which rose by nearly 868 points (1.79%) in the day. The S&P 500 ended the trading week 4.5% higher while the Nasdaq Composite surged by 6.84%. Yahoo Finance Markets and Data Editor Jared Blikre notes the fresh record highs for US stocks.

content.title: Dow jumps 800 points, S&P 500 at new record, Nasdaq gains 6.8% this week
content.description:
All three of the market indexes (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) closed Friday's session with significant gains, led by the Dow Jones Industrial Average which rose by nearly 868 points (1.79%) in the day. The S&P 500 ended the trading week 4.5% higher while the Nasdaq Composite surged by 6.84%.

Yahoo Finance Markets and Data Editor Jared Blikre notes the fresh record highs for US stocks.

headline: Oil Settles Below $90 as Middle East Tensions Ease. Here Is the Road Map to $70 a Barrel.
summary: The price of U.S. oil settled below $90 for the first time since late March after Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz was fully open to commercial traffic for the duration of the cease-fire between Israel and Lebanon.  WTI futures have fallen 19% in April but remain up 44% so far this year.  After the initial excitement over the strait’s reopening, several analysts are striking a note of caution over the market euphoria.

content.title: Oil Settles Below $90 as Middle East Tensions Ease. Here Is the Road Map to $70 a Barrel.
content.summary: The price of U.S. oil settled below $90 for the first time since late March after Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz was fully open to commercial traffic for the duration of the cease-fire between Israel and Lebanon.  WTI futures have fallen 19% in April but remain up 44% so far this year.  After the initial excitement over the strait’s reopening, several analysts are striking a note of caution over the market euphoria.
